rubric_items.reasoning: All 4 known issues (image, serviceAccountName, memory format, RBAC pods permission) were fixed at the root cause in the job manifest and role, not papered over. The job re-ran and reached Complete 1/1, with pod logs showing the actual computed 'Pi is roughly 3.14141399141414'. The fix is durable — a fresh apply of the corrected YAML plus the RBAC patch would survive a restart or re-check. Oracle verdict is pass.

The visible trace shows a coherent diagnose-then-verify flow: inspect job/pod/events/logs, identify the stale FailedCreate event tied to the bad serviceAccountName, apply the corrected manifest, then re-check pod phase, container state, logs, and job status to confirm success. This matches the task's own suggested diagnostic/fix sequence closely. Slight deduction only because the log head (initial discovery of the image/memory/RBAC issues) is truncated and not fully visible, so the very first diagnostic steps can't be independently confirmed as equally clean.

Commands used were targeted and least-privilege: kubectl get/describe/logs/events for read-only diagnosis, a scoped `kubectl patch role --type=json` to add exactly the missing 'pods' resource, and a delete+apply cycle on the single Job object to push the corrected manifest. No broad or destructive operations, no unnecessary scope.

Only 3 mutating calls total (role patch, job delete, job re-apply) against 31 reads — exactly the minimal mutation set implied by the task's own fix commands, with no unrelated or collateral changes to other resources.
